attributes.description: Alerts when both inbound and outbound network bytes are near zero, which may indicate a dead application even when status checks still pass.
attributes.name: [AWS EC2 OTel] Network traffic drop

investigation_guide.blob:
## EC2 Network Traffic Drop

### Meaning
An instance with near-zero network traffic may have a hung or crashed application while still passing EC2 status checks. This rule flags instances where both ingress and egress bytes are below the configured threshold.

### Triage
1. Verify the instance is expected to have network activity (exclude intentionally idle or stopped workloads).
2. Check application health endpoints and process status on the instance.
3. Compare against historical baseline — tune the byte threshold to avoid alerting on legitimately quiet instances.
4. Review status check metrics — instance may need reboot if the application is unresponsive.

esqlQuery.esql:
FROM metrics-awscloudwatchreceiver.otel-*
| WHERE attributes.Namespace == "AWS/EC2"
    AND attributes.MetricName IN ("NetworkIn", "NetworkOut")
    AND attributes.stat == "Sum"
| EVAL ec2_instance = CONCAT(attributes.InstanceId, " (", resource.attributes.cloud.region, ")")
| STATS
    net_in = SUM(`metrics.amazonaws.com/AWS/EC2/NetworkIn`) WHERE attributes.MetricName == "NetworkIn",
    net_out = SUM(`metrics.amazonaws.com/AWS/EC2/NetworkOut`) WHERE attributes.MetricName == "NetworkOut"
  BY ec2_instance
// Low-traffic threshold (bytes): tune to exclude legitimately idle instances
| WHERE net_in < 1024 AND net_out < 1024
| SORT net_in ASC
| LIMIT 100
